title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. plicata
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. plicata, also known as the Alpine Lady's Mantle, is a subspecies of the common alchemilla plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This perennial plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. plicata include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
